constraints: Reveal rooms and connections through exploration
Do not reveal undiscovered secrets
Keep guidance light and avoid GPS-style navigation
Target skill-based top-down combat players
Support casual through mastery players with meaningful difficulty tailoring
Do not rely only on health and damage multipliers
Complete MVP campaign saves progression and difficulty work offline
Multiplayer follows only after a successful MVP
Preserve clean extension boundaries without MVP networking complexity
Co-op campaign combat exploration objectives and bosses are the first multiplayer direction
Leaderboards remain post-MVP
No networking scaling shared progression or leaderboard service in MVP
Windows PC polished MVP first
Keyboard mouse and gamepad support
Android follows after PC stability
Keep actions platform-neutral UI scalable and saves mobile-compatible
Exploratory first clear roughly 45 to 75 minutes
Mastered run roughly 20 to 35 minutes
Natural checkpoint sections roughly 10 to 20 minutes
Prioritize distinct replayable content over filler
Core layouts bosses major encounters and competitive categories remain deterministic
Allow bounded authored modifiers and variants
Preserve mastery and fair speedrunning
Do not convert campaign into a heavily randomized roguelike
Weapons mainly come from floor pickups and tiered random strongboxes
Shops provide a randomized purchasable inventory
Random discovery is a central source of excitement
Random results are bounded by progression
Duplicates remain possible and useful
Shops may expose roughly 40 progression-appropriate slots
Weapon types enter the pool gradually
Boxes use nearby progression windows
Stars augments and enchantments create variants
Use soft protection against pathological bad luck
Weapon families return as authored stronger successors
Successors may add modest mechanical changes
Complex and inherently stronger archetypes unlock later
Deliberate power inflation is planned and paced
Late archetypes must add novelty complexity or power
Shop stock remains stable until deliberately refreshed
Rerolls are limited and increasingly expensive
A small number of items may be locked through rerolls
Prevent cheap infinite perfect-roll fishing
Current direction uses eight strongbox tiers and two-star ten-level augments
Elite high-augment gear primarily comes from high-tier floor boxes
Exact weapon and enchantments remain random
Extreme shop rolls occur only at screenshot-worthy rarity
High box tiers unlock progressively
Exact odds require playtesting
Player level and campaign progress hard-cap eligible box tiers
Difficulty objectives bosses and mastery improve high-tier odds
Every level retains a non-zero chance for unlocked tiers
Earlier levels have lower odds and weaker quality distributions
Later difficult content provides materially better expected rewards
Each box tier has a quality floor and weighted expected band
Neighboring tiers overlap with limited jackpot tails
Older lower-base-level weapons may roll excellent stars and augments
Balance base level family power modifiers and synergy together
Low-tier farming must not overpower current progression
Exceptional modifiers may bridge several progression levels
Strong successors eventually overtake older jackpots
New weapons remain meaningful
Older weapons retain situational value through handling effects matchups or synergy
No permanent universal best-in-slot outliers
Comparison UI communicates contextual strengths
Ordinary shop weapons are attainable but consequential purchases
Stars augments enchantments and later families sharply raise prices
Jackpots may require several runs of savings
Selling and dismantling return partial value
Purchases rerolls and services compete for core currency
Exact prices and income require playtesting
One consumed token reserves one exact shop item
The reservation survives rerolls visits and completed runs
Only one profile-wide reservation exists
Reserved item keeps its original price
No expiry until purchase or deliberate discard
Ordinary reroll locks remain separate and short-term
Unwanted weapons may be sold for currency or dismantled for bounded materials
Material yields reflect level rarity stars augments and enchantment categories
Materials support controlled improvement rerolling utility crafting or later systems
Do not allow free perfect-item manufacturing
Both paths return partial value and resist farming exploits
Provide favourites locks bulk marking auto-junk sorting comparison and keep-best-copy tools
Do not make direct duplicate fusion the primary upgrade path
Use one profile-wide universal weapon inventory or armory
Capacity is generous but finite and may be expanded through progression upgrades
Shop purchases enter usable inventory immediately and can power up the player mid-run
Strongboxes earned during a round remain sealed and are opened after the round
Do not let lucky high-tier box contents instantly trivialize the remaining run
Overflow handling must avoid silent deletion and remain Android-friendly
Reaching a checkpoint permanently secures all boxes collected since the previous checkpoint
Secured boxes survive later death abandonment or exit
Only boxes collected after the latest checkpoint remain at risk
All secured boxes stay sealed until the run ends
Each checkpoint section carries extraction tension without erasing an entire long run
Completion and later-section incentives must discourage trivial bank-and-exit farming
Secured boxes enter the reward vault in exact collection order
Default reveal order preserves pickup order
Individual openings may use full presentation
Batch openings remain fast but resolve in collection order
Opened items enter a temporary capacity-safe review tray
Players may compare favourite keep sell or dismantle rewards
No reward is silently deleted because normal inventory is full
Players may leave the vault with unresolved opened rewards
Pending rewards remain exactly rolled in a persistent capacity-exempt inbox
The inbox survives menus closure crashes and save reloads
Resolve the inbox before opening more boxes or beginning another reward-generating run
No unresolved reward is silently deleted or auto-converted in MVP
Provide fast bulk actions and clear blocking messages
After MVP add opt-in configurable auto-processing with strong protection for favourites locked exceptional and contextual rolls
Commit an immutable seed tier source level progression difficulty modifiers collection order and loot-table version on pickup
Opening always produces the same exact item regardless of later progression settings updates delays or reloads
Consume the box and persist the generated reward through one crash-safe atomic transaction
Reloading during or after reveal resumes the same reward and cannot reroll or duplicate it
Each box identifier may grant its reward only once
Automated deterministic reload interruption and duplicate-grant tests are required
Keep campaign progression inventory economy and strongboxes fully playable offline
Use checksums or signatures transaction journals rotating backups and monotonic counters for important grants and economy actions
Detect suspicious rollback or mismatched save history while prioritizing safe recovery from corruption crashes and interrupted writes
Suspicious or manually altered local state may mark a profile unverified without blocking offline campaign play
Future leaderboards official speedrun events and competitive challenges use a separate verified-run system rather than server-authoritative campaign progression
Accept that determined users can ultimately alter a fully local game and focus MVP effort on preventing casual rollback and duplication abuse
The game is free to download and enter
Real-money spending does not alter progression combat power loot quality drop rates difficulty or competitive performance
Sell optional skins and visual variants for existing mechs weapons effects and comparable content
Do not sell strongboxes weapons stars augments enchantments currencies materials rerolls reservation tokens inventory capacity levels or progression advantages
Randomized strongboxes remain gameplay-earned and are not real-money loot boxes
Free and paying players share the same gameplay balance reward tables progression difficulty and competitive rules
Cosmetics must preserve combat and telegraph readability
Every campaign level boss weapon family difficulty progression system challenge and gameplay update is free
No authored gameplay is placed behind paid expansions starter paywalls or a one-time full-game unlock
Revenue comes from direct-purchase cosmetics and cosmetic supporter bundles only
Paid offerings contain no exclusive gameplay functionality or progression value
Free and paying players always share playable levels modes co-op content challenges weapons and balance rules
Do not weaken free progression or introduce paid advantages to solve revenue pressure
Players can begin continue and complete offline gameplay as guests without login
Login never gates quick play campaign progression inventory or difficulty
An optional account may link cosmetic entitlements loadouts favourites cloud backup device migration and eventual PC-to-Android ownership portability
Original-platform purchases remain restorable from valid storefront receipts without requiring the optional account
Account linkage must not make gameplay progression or offline access always-online
Collect only necessary account data and support recovery unlinking and clearly explained entitlement merging
Android portability is post-PC and not an MVP release blocker
Linked players may upload and restore complete gameplay-profile snapshots while offline guest play remains unrestricted
Never merge individual weapons currencies strongboxes inventory transactions or progression events from divergent saves
Require the player to choose one complete local or cloud save lineage with clear timestamp level playtime campaign inventory and verification summaries
Retain the discarded lineage temporarily as a recovery backup
Use profile lineage identifiers transaction journals monotonic grant counters and save versioning to prevent duplication across branch choices
Prepare identifiers schemas and versioning during PC development but deliver the cross-platform service post-MVP with Android
Do not implement automatic item-by-item merging

recovered_state.rule: Re-verify reconstructed details before final requirements

open_questions: Death checkpoint respawn and world-reset behavior
Exact MVP enemy roster weapon list and boss content
Stack and technical architecture after product scope is mature

readiness.reason: High-weight Product Discovery decisions and recovery verification remain incomplete

next_action.instruction: On resume, ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether cleared rooms remain cleared and only the death room resets, the current checkpoint section resets deterministically while earlier checkpoint-banked sections stay cleared, or enemies may dynamically reclaim previously cleared rooms after death. Preserve checkpoint-banked strongboxes, fixed handcrafted teleport checkpoints, deterministic campaign mastery, difficulty-specific recovery rules, and quit-anytime single-use suspend-save intent from the recovered draft without presenting unverified details as final. Include repetition risk, challenge tension, speedrunning consistency, implementation complexity, and difficulty scaling. Put the agent recommendation after all options at the end of the card. Persist the accepted answer and update the handoff before continuing.
